evidence.scientific_clarity: Brief asks for 6-cluster ridgeline with KDE and left-axis labels; script delivers exactly that with overlap=0.6 and viridis-colored stacked densities.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; PDF confirms Type3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans glyphs — text is not selectable or editable.
evidence.palette: Viridis sequential colormap is colorblind-safe and appropriate for ordered cluster data; no red/green directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: overlap=0.6 gives mild ridge overlap as briefed; tight_layout() used; clean spines; xlim padded just enough for left-side labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and states ordering criterion (median expression), but cites no actual numerical median values per cluster.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is 46 KB and present, but fonts are embedded as Type3 (confirmed via strings); fonttype=42 was never set in the script.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (46 KB), figure.png (79 KB), caption.tex (397 B), gen_figure.py (1.7 KB); bonus figure.svg also saved.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains KDE bandwidth, stacking, and ordering rationale with a qualitative finding (progressive shift), but omits actual median values that would anchor the claim numerically.

overall_strengths: Viridis colormap is colorblind-safe and semantically correct for ordered cluster data.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; layout is clean with appropriate ridge overlap and minimal chartjunk.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) never set, so all text in figure.pdf and figure.svg is rendered as Type3 bitmap glyphs — not selectable, not editable, and will look poor at high zoom.
Caption lacks concrete numerical anchors (e.g., actual median expression values per cluster), weakening the specific scientific claim.
